extraction.one_line_summary: GALL Felix attacked from reduced peloton with teammate DEWULF's assistance on final climb, won by 1:03 and took yellow jersey by 2 seconds over SKJELMOSE.
tactical_patterns: AG2R used DEWULF from breakaway as bridge domestique to pace GALL after his attack from favorites group.
Trek-Segafredo permitted MÄDER at only 2 minutes GC to escape, suggesting comfort with non-threat breakaway riders.
BARDET attacked multiple times in final kilometer but others marked him rather than collaborating, gifting GALL time.
Bonus seconds became decisive with GALL winning yellow by only 2 seconds; EVENEPOEL's 6-second haul for second salvaged GC.

rider_signals.GALL Felix: positive: converted multiple recent second-places into breakthrough win when AG2R provided breakaway-to-attack tactical support
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: went backwards early on final climb hardest section, dropped from yellow jersey contention to sixth overall
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: dropped multiple times but fought back repeatedly showing resilience over pure climbing strength on gradients
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: positive: most aggressive seen in long while with repeated late attacks though marked by rivals
rider_signals.SKJELMOSE Mattias: positive: secured third place and 4 bonus seconds to finish 2 seconds off yellow, positioned perfectly
